hero.headline: Democracy is daily, not just on election day.
hero.sub: CivicOS keeps the public record of citizen action between elections — issues reported, petitions signed, responses received — on your street, in your community, today.
hero.trust: Open source · Verified accounts · Audit-trailed in UTC

parties.title: Built for everyone in public life

citizens.title: Make your voice count, every day
citizens.body: Report issues on your street, sign petitions for what you care about, follow your representatives, and see what gets done — in days, not election cycles.

reps.title: Show your work in public
reps.body: Respond to your constituents in one place. Your response rate sits on your public profile — accountability is the design, not a feature.

government.title: A trusted channel to the public
government.body: Route citizen reports to the right office, publish initiatives, and demonstrate progress with a verifiable, timestamped record.

ngos.title: Mobilise around real evidence
ngos.body: Aggregate community signals, organise petitions, and measure impact with structured civic data instead of anecdote.

articles.title: Continuous participation, not just at election time

issues.title: Report and track issues
issues.body: Pothole, streetlight, unsafe road — file it in three minutes with photos and follow the status from open to under review to resolved.

petitions.title: Start and sign petitions
petitions.body: Goal-based petitions with automatic milestones at 25%, 50%, and 100%. One citizen, one signature, no inflated numbers.

reps.title: Follow your representatives
reps.body: See who represents you, what they have said, and how quickly they respond — all on a public profile that updates itself.

discover.title: Discover what matters nearby
discover.body: A feed ranked by proximity: your community first, then your LGA, then your state — so local always reads first.

notifications.title: Stay in the loop in real time
notifications.body: Notifications stream live the moment an issue updates, a petition crosses a threshold, or your representative replies.

transparency.title: Transparency by default
transparency.body: Every interaction is timestamped and traceable. Public information stays public; personal data stays private.

principles.title: Five principles, written before the code
principles.note: Just as digital banking modernised how we move money, CivicOS modernises how we participate in democracy — transparent, accessible, and built to last.

steps.title: From sign-up to civic action in under five minutes

join.title: Join the register
join.body: Sign up with your name and email. Identity-verified, no paperwork, no fees.

pick.title: Pick your patch
pick.body: Select your state and LGA so we know what counts as "near you" in your feed.

act.title: Take action
act.body: Report an issue, sign a petition, follow your representatives — your name, your record.

follow.title: See what gets done
follow.body: Real-time updates as status changes, responses arrive, and milestones land.

cost.a: Yes. CivicOS is free for citizens — forever. Reporting issues, signing petitions, following your representatives, and receiving updates cost nothing. Public institutions and NGOs may have paid tiers for advanced tooling, but the citizen experience is free.

privacy.a: Your email, phone number, and address are private and never shown publicly. Only your display name, role, and the public actions you take (issues you raise, petitions you sign, comments you post) are visible to other users. You control what you share.

reps.q: Do representatives actually use the platform?
reps.a: Representatives are onboarded community by community. Their response rate is visible on their public profile — accountability is built into the design, so silence is also a signal.

coverage.q: What if no one represents my area yet?
coverage.a: You can still join, pick your community, and file. Once a representative for your constituency joins, your existing activity links to them automatically.

ownership.q: Who built CivicOS — and who owns it?
ownership.a: CivicOS is open civic infrastructure, built in the open and stewarded by an independent team. The codebase is public, the architecture is documented, and contributions are welcome. No political party, single funder, or commercial interest controls the platform.

abuse.q: How do you prevent abuse, spam, or misinformation?
abuse.a: Every account is identity-verified at sign-up. Comments and submissions are rate-limited, moderated against community guidelines, and traceable to a real account. Petitions require unique signatures — one citizen, one signature.

newsletter.title: Subscribe to The Dispatch — be first to know when CivicOS lands in your area
newsletter.sub: One short email per month with product updates, community milestones, and rollout news. No marketing fluff. Unsubscribe anytime.
newsletter.doneSub: We'll be in touch as we expand coverage.

cta.title: Ready to take part?
cta.sub: Join citizens, representatives, and organisations building a more accountable civic life — together, on the record.

footer.mission: Strengthening democratic participation through secure, transparent, accessible digital infrastructure.

terms.eyebrow: The Public Record — Terms of Service
terms.title: Terms of service
terms.intro: These are the rules for using CivicOS. Plain English, no fine print, and specific to how the platform actually works. Where privacy is covered separately, see the <privacy>privacy notice</privacy>.

who.title: Who we are
who.body: CivicOS is open civic infrastructure operated at civicos.ng — a public register for citizen action between elections. In these terms, "we" is CivicOS; "you" is anyone who signs up, reads, reports, signs, or comments here.

what.title: What CivicOS is (and isn't)
what.body: CivicOS is an informational platform for civic engagement. It lets citizens report community issues, sign petitions, follow representatives, and see public responses from organizations. It is not a substitute for emergency services, legal advice, or government complaint channels. If you or someone else is in danger, call the appropriate emergency number, not CivicOS.

eligibility.title: Who can use CivicOS
eligibility.body: CivicOS is intended for people 18 and older. You need a valid email address and, in most cases, a community in Nigeria you're associated with. Representatives, government administrators, NGO staff, and organization members hold elevated accounts — those roles are granted, not self-claimed.

account.title: Your account
account.body: One account per person. You're responsible for keeping your password safe; we secure passwords with bcrypt and never store them in the clear (see the privacy notice for details). You must give truthful information — impersonating another person, a representative, or an organization is grounds for immediate account termination.

content.title: Content you post
content.body:
Everything you file — issues, petitions, comments — becomes part of the public record when it's public by design. You keep authorship, but you grant CivicOS a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to display and share your public contributions for the purpose of running the platform.

You are responsible for what you post. Do not publish anything you don't have the right to share, do not include private information about others without consent, and understand that anything public may be quoted, screenshotted, or shared.

conduct.title: Prohibited conduct
conduct.body:
You may not use CivicOS to: post spam or repetitive commercial content; harass, threaten, or dox other people; publish content that incites violence; publish demonstrably false claims about identifiable people or events; impersonate anyone; evade a moderation decision by creating another account; scrape at rates that impact service for others.

Filing false reports, upvoting your own content through multiple accounts, or coordinating inauthentic activity are also prohibited.

moderation.title: Moderation
moderation.body: Moderators may hide content, remove it entirely, or restrict accounts that violate these terms. Every moderation decision is logged to an audit trail that CivicOS staff can review. If you believe a decision was wrong, write to hello@civicos.ng with the content ID and your reasoning; we will review, but final decisions rest with the moderation team.

reps.title: Representatives and organizations
reps.body: Accounts marked as representatives or verified organizations carry public trust — citizens use the badge to distinguish real officials from impersonators. Misusing a verified account — pretending to speak for the person or org, posting non-civic commercial content, or coordinating to deceive citizens — will cause the verified status to be revoked and, in serious cases, the account terminated.

petitions.title: Petitions
petitions.body: Petitions on CivicOS are civic voice, not legal instruments. Signing a petition does not compel any government body to act, and hitting a signature target does not create any obligation. Petitions still matter — they surface priorities to elected representatives, journalists, and the public — but do not treat them as binding decisions.

availability.title: Availability and changes
availability.body:
We do our best to keep CivicOS reliable but we make no uptime guarantee. Planned maintenance, outages, or intentional degradations (e.g. rate limits during abuse spikes) may make the platform temporarily unavailable.

We may change these terms. Material changes will be posted on this page and, where they affect what you can do with your account, notified to registered users by email at least 14 days before they take effect. The effective date at the top of these terms always reflects the current version.

termination.title: Termination
termination.body:
You can leave CivicOS at any time — the delete-account button in your profile anonymizes your public contributions and purges your PII within 30 days (see privacy §7 for the exact retention picture).

We may suspend or terminate an account that violates these terms. In most cases we will attempt to notify you and explain the reason. In cases involving credible threats, coordinated abuse, or legal risk, we may act immediately without notice.

law.title: Governing law
law.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. Disputes arising out of your use of CivicOS will be resolved in the courts of Lagos State unless a different venue is required by law.

privacy.title: Privacy notice
privacy.intro: This is CivicOS's privacy notice. Plain English, no legalese, and specific to how CivicOS actually works. If anything below is unclear, write to us — the address is in §12.

who.title: Who we are
who.body: CivicOS is open civic infrastructure — a public register for citizen action between elections. This notice covers the CivicOS platform and any successor domain. "We" is CivicOS; "you" is anyone who registers, reports, signs, comments, or reads.

what.title: What we collect
what.body:
Account information. Your email, a password (never stored in clear — see §6), your display name, your community (state and LGA), and optionally your phone number and avatar. Representatives and admins may store additional public contact fields (office email, phone, website) tied to their public role.

Activity. Issues you report, petitions you sign or create, comments you post, representatives you follow, notifications delivered to you, and timestamps for each — all in UTC.

Security signals. IP address, request path, session ID, and rate-limit counters. Kept briefly, then discarded (see §7).

We do not collect. Real-time location, contacts, biometrics, browsing history outside CivicOS, or advertising identifiers.

why.title: Why we collect it
why.body: To let you participate — report issues, sign petitions, follow representatives. To notify you when things you care about change — an issue you upvoted moves to RESOLVED, a petition you signed hits a milestone. To keep the public record honest — every action is audit-trailed so admins can investigate abuse. To keep you signed in without asking for your password every time.

donot.title: What we do not do
donot.body: We do not sell your data. Ever. To anyone. We do not run advertising, tracking pixels, or cross-site cookies. We do not enrich your profile from external databases. We do not embed third-party analytics scripts that could exfiltrate your activity.

sharing.title: What is public, what is private
sharing.body:
Public by design: your display name, community, petitions you author, comments you post publicly on issues, petitions, and representatives, and aggregate counts (signature totals, upvotes, follower counts).

Private by default: your email, phone, password hash, private notifications, security signals, and the audit trail of admin actions on your account.

Petition signatures appear as counts, not names, on public pages — unless you explicitly opt into signed public endorsement.

security.title: Security
security.body: Passwords are hashed with bcrypt at cost 12 — even we cannot recover them. Access tokens are short-lived JWTs; refresh tokens are opaque 32-byte hex values stored SHA-256-hashed, with family-based replay detection. All traffic is TLS. Admin actions are audit-logged. Report a vulnerability to security@civicos.ng.

retention.title: Retention
retention.body:
Active account. We keep your account and activity for as long as your account is active.

Deleted account. On request, we anonymise your public contributions (comments show as removed; petitions you authored show that the author withdrew) and purge your personal information within 30 days. Aggregate counts stay, for record integrity.

Security signals. IP addresses and rate-limit counters expire within 24 hours.

Backups. Encrypted, retained 30 days, then destroyed.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body:
You can — at any time, without asking permission — access, export, correct, or delete your data. Log in and use the Profile page for access, correction, and export. To delete your account, use "Delete account" in Profile, or write to privacy@civicos.ng.

If you are a Nigerian resident, you have rights under the NDPR (Nigeria Data Protection Regulation). If you are resident elsewhere, you have the rights of your local jurisdiction (GDPR if in the EU, and so on) in addition to your NDPR rights as a Nigerian citizen.

cookies.title: Cookies and storage
cookies.body: We store your refresh token in an httpOnly cookie so you stay signed in. We store your access token, language preference, and dismissed-banner state in browser localStorage. That is it — no analytics cookies, no tracking cookies, no third-party cookies.

children.title: Children
children.body: CivicOS is intended for people 18 and older. If we learn we have collected data from a minor, we delete it.

changes.title: Changes to this notice
changes.body: We will post material changes on this page and notify all account holders by email at least 14 days before they take effect. The effective date at the top of this notice always reflects the current version.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body:
privacy@civicos.ng — our privacy team.

security@civicos.ng — for security disclosures.

CivicOS · Nigeria

register.cardSubtitle: It takes less than a minute to get started.

fields.title: Title
fields.bioPlaceholder: What should citizens know about your role?

organization.title: Organization application
organization.sub: Add the organization details that the admin team should verify before approval.

onboarding.title: Let's get you set up.
onboarding.description: Pick your community so your feed, notifications, and representatives all point to your patch. You can change this later from your profile.
onboarding.stateHint: Choose the state you want to be active in on CivicOS.
onboarding.communityHint: These are the CivicOS communities set up in {{lga}}, {{state}}. Choose the one closest to where you live or care about most.
onboarding.communityEmptySub: Nobody has created a CivicOS community for {{lga}}, {{state}} yet. You can still browse the platform — the yellow banner on Discover will remind you to come back when one appears.

forgot.title: Forgot your password?
forgot.description: Enter the email you signed up with. We'll send a link to set a new password.
forgot.cardSubtitle: The link is good for one hour.
forgot.sentSub: If an account exists for {{email}}, a reset link is on its way. Give it a minute — check spam too.
forgot.unknownEmailNote: For your security we don't confirm whether an email is registered.

reset.title: Set a new password.
reset.description: Pick something you haven't used elsewhere. Minimum 8 characters.
reset.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
reset.successSub: You're signed in. Time to get back to what matters.
reset.expiredSub: Reset links last 1 hour. Request a new one to continue.
reset.invalidTitle: This reset link is no longer valid.
reset.invalidSub: It may have already been used. Request a new one if you still need to reset.

verify.sentSub: We sent a verification link to {{email}}. Click it to confirm your account — the link is good for 24 hours.
verify.sentTip: Don't see it? Check spam, or use the button below to resend.
verify.pendingApproval: Once your email is verified, your representative or organization request will wait for admin approval before elevated access is granted.
verify.successSub: You're all set. You can now file issues, sign petitions, and join the conversation.
verify.invalidTitle: This verification link is no longer valid.
verify.expiredSub: Verification links last 24 hours. Sign in and request a new one from your profile.
verify.invalidSub: It may have already been used. Sign in and request a new one if your email isn't verified yet.
verify.bannerTitle: Verify your email to unlock everything.
verify.bannerSub: We sent a link to {{email}}. Until you click it, you can browse but not file, sign, or comment.
verify.bannerCompact: Verify your email to file, sign, and comment.
verify.actionRequired: Verify your email to upvote, sign petitions, and join the conversation. Check your inbox — or go to Profile to resend the verification email.

manifesto.headline: An operating system for civic participation.
manifesto.body: CivicOS is shared digital infrastructure that lets governments, universities, NGOs and communities build trusted civic experiences — organized around the places people actually live.
